briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 Zoning Bylaw with new 2026 version
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replace it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, this would change the rules for what you can build or do on your property. The decision has not been made yet — this is up for discussion and approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules would affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the Authority run its housing programs for another year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will vote on whether to approve the funding at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby, regional, and BIA
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) purposes. A BIA is a local area where businesses pay extra taxes to fund improvements like street cleaning, marketing, or events. If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 for city services, regional services (like Metro Vancouver), and any BIA your business is in.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — these rates will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve certain land-related documents, like subdivision plans. This is a routine administrative appointment that helps keep property development and land transactions moving smoothly in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the city's zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the bylaw normally allows. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council meeting date — the decision could affect your own building plans or property values.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on insurance contract renewals
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This decision would approve the renewal of insurance coverage for city-owned buildings, vehicles, and operations. If approved, the new contracts would take effect for the 2026 policy year. The decision does not directly change any rules for businesses, but it affects how the city manages its own risk and liability.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would help members learn about best practices and network with other heritage professionals. A decision has not yet been made.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to buy the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine purchasing decision that keeps city technology working, but it does not directly change any rules or fees for local businesses.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for the new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the next council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the city's contract for external audit services. This is not a final decision yet. If approved, the change would modify the existing agreement with the company that audits the city's financial statements. The purpose is to ensure the city's financial records are reviewed properly. The specific details of the contract change are on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you — this is an internal city contract decision. Watch for the council vote if you are a contractor or supplier to the project.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant program. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the grants would provide funding to local community groups and non-profits. The vote will happen at an upcoming council meeting. Business owners who partner with or rely on community organizations should watch for the outcome, as it could affect local programs and services.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby business licences
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change certain fees for business licences, permits, and other city services. The changes are not yet approved — they are up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting. If passed, the new fees would take effect later in 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed fee schedule — your business licence costs could change.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, such as limits on rent increases above the provincial guideline and stronger protections against evictions for renovations. The goal is to help renters stay in their homes. Council will consider endorsing the framework and the bylaw at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal that council must vote on.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if passed, this bylaw will affect how you manage rental properties in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety features along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect local traffic and parking.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on nearby traffic and parking.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design rules for safer intersections
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new street designs to include 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer layout that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles using curbs and barriers. If approved, this would apply to all new development and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this will affect design requirements for any new development or road project in the Town Centre.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will receive a staff report on April 28, 2026, recommending against exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. Staff state the City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH and that Restrictive Covenants are private interests not recognized as exemptions. A proposed alternative — amending the Building Bylaw city-wide to require compliance with Restrictive Covenants at building permit stage — is not recommended due to added costs, process delays, and impacts on residential, commercial, and industrial properties across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on the staff recommendation at the April 28, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — staff report on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption

briefs.source_body: 9.1 BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements. Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information-only item — no vote or decision is required. The report gives a snapshot of construction activity in the city, including the number and types of permits issued. It does not propose any new rules or changes.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a report that recommends formally recognizing the heritage value of the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. The report includes an 'as-found' condition report and a 'Statement of Significance' — a document that explains why the building is historically important. If Council approves this, the property could be added to the city's heritage register, which may affect future renovations, demolitions, or redevelopment plans. The decision has not been made yet; Council is reviewing the information first.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage this property, watch for the Council decision — heritage listing could limit future changes to the building.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed last year. It is a review of current operations, not a proposed new bylaw or fee change. No decisions on new rules or costs are being voted on at this time.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will outline current and planned actions to improve food security and reduce waste in the city. The report is for information only and does not include a vote on new rules at this time. However, it may lead to future bylaws or programs that could affect local food businesses, including restaurants, grocery stores, and urban farms.
briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Monitor future council meetings for potential new food sustainability rules.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Safety upgrades at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A vote on whether to move forward with these changes is expected at an upcoming council meeting. If approved, the improvements could affect traffic flow and access for businesses and visitors near the Metrotown area.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the council decision — if approved, prepare for potential traffic disruptions near your business.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby rezoning to add hotel, restaurant, and 362 more homes at Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning amendment for 3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Road, and 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place. The developer wants to change a previous approval (from 2024) that allowed two residential towers with office and retail space. The new plan would replace some office space with a hotel and restaurants, add 362 more market strata units (condos you can buy), and make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller. The developer also proposes to meet affordable housing requirements for Phase 1 only, by moving that obligation to a different site (the Carrigan site). This is a proposal only — nothing has been approved yet.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote to share your views.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would allow a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. The plan includes 290 residential units, ground-floor commercial space facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal is consistent with the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. If approved, the project would bring new housing and retail to the area.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building to be built on the site. The proposal follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan (OCP) — the long-term plan for how the city should grow — and uses special transition rules that were approved for this type of project. This is not a final decision yet; Council will consider the rezoning at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — you may want to attend or submit comments to Council.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot program bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, the pilot would set new rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets and paths. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters could affect your business operations or deliveries.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes and scooters in Burnaby parks.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter enforcement
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. If approved, the bylaw would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for things like improper parking, riding on sidewalks, or other violations by e-bike and e-scooter users. The amendment is needed to support the city's new e-bike and e-scooter pilot program, which is testing how these devices can be used safely on Burnaby streets.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rentals operate in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates up for council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all land and buildings (called 'improvements') in the city. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of BC's Community Charter — those are the provincial rules that tell municipalities how to set property taxes. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 based on your property's assessed value. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments if you want to have a say on the proposed 2026 tax rates.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
